message:
fix(writing): reconcile setup-duration copy, drop dead Code helper, tighten e2e markers

Three post-restructure cleanups surfaced by a fresh-eyes review pass.

1) Setup duration mismatch between article and InstallFlowViz.
   Article copy (TOC link, SectionHeader, body paragraph, closing
   pattern CTA) consistently says "twenty minutes". InstallFlowViz h3
   said "about fifteen minutes" and its closing ribbon said "About 15
   minutes". Both now say twenty to match the article. Individual step
   times (5+5+3+2=15) remain as-is — "about twenty" reads as a
   conservative estimate that tolerates the variance.

2) Dead Code helper in wills-estate-article.tsx removed. It was used
   by the old INSTALL section's three-tab layout with bash snippets;
   the restructured setup section uses numbered prose + InstallFlowViz
   instead. Zero JSX references remained.

3) E2E viz content markers tightened. Three of the seven visualization
   markers were /./ (matches any single character, effectively no
   assertion). Tightened to stable rendered copy:
   - install-flow: h3 "What you need (about twenty minutes of setup)"
   - working-folder: h3 "Put your real documents in one folder,
     point the desktop app at it"
   - stack: eyebrow pill "Agent + Skill Stack"
   Markers verified against the current viz bodies.

bun tsc --noEmit clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

- What you need (about fifteen minutes of setup) + What you need (about twenty minutes of setup)

Four steps, each one a few minutes. No terminal, no code, no @@ -3941,7 +3941,7 @@ export function InstallFlowViz() {

- About 15 minutes. Then you're done setting up. + About 20 minutes. Then you're done setting up.
